title: Operations: Maintenance Schedule, Equipment, Checklists & Parts

nextDueCell.js: (()=>{const tr=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].find(t=>/Lubricate conveyor chain/.test(t.textContent||''));return tr?tr.querySelectorAll('td')[6]:null})()

statusRepairCell.js: (()=>{const tr=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].find(t=>/Chemical Pump Array/.test(t.textContent||''));if(!tr)return null;const c=tr.querySelectorAll('td');return c[c.length-1]})()

dailyOpenComplCell.js: (()=>{const tr=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].find(t=>/Daily Opening Checklist/.test(t.textContent||''));if(!tr)return null;const c=tr.querySelectorAll('td');return c[c.length-1]})()

weeklySafetyComplCell.js: (()=>{const tr=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].find(t=>/Weekly Safety Inspection/.test(t.textContent||''));if(!tr)return null;const c=tr.querySelectorAll('td');return c[c.length-1]})()

predicates.scheduleLoaded: (()=>{const rows=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0);return location.pathname.endsWith('/maint-schedule.html')&&[...document.querySelectorAll('.a-metric')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0).length>=4&&rows.length>=3&&[...document.querySelectorAll('table thead th')].some(e=>/^Next Due$/i.test((e.textContent||'').trim()))&&!/Couldn.t load|Failed to fetch|HTTP Error 404/i.test(document.body.textContent||'')})()
predicates.equipmentLoaded: (()=>{const rows=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0);return location.pathname.endsWith('/maint-equipment.html')&&[...document.querySelectorAll('.a-metric')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0).length>=4&&rows.length>=3&&[...document.querySelectorAll('table thead th')].some(e=>/^Status$/i.test((e.textContent||'').trim()))&&!/Couldn.t load|Failed to fetch|HTTP Error 404/i.test(document.body.textContent||'')})()
predicates.checklistLoaded: (()=>{const rows=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0);return location.pathname.endsWith('/maint-checklist.html')&&[...document.querySelectorAll('.a-metric')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0).length>=4&&rows.length>=3&&[...document.querySelectorAll('table thead th')].some(e=>/^Completion/i.test((e.textContent||'').trim()))&&!/Couldn.t load|Failed to fetch|HTTP Error 404/i.test(document.body.textContent||'')})()
predicates.partsLoaded: (()=>{const rows=[...document.querySelectorAll('table tbody tr')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0);return location.pathname.endsWith('/maint-parts.html')&&[...document.querySelectorAll('.a-metric')].filter(e=>e.getBoundingClientRect().width>0).length>=4&&rows.length>=3&&[...document.querySelectorAll('table thead th')].some(e=>/^Qty on Hand$/i.test((e.textContent||'').trim()))&&!/Couldn.t load|Failed to fetch|HTTP Error 404/i.test(document.body.textContent||'')})()

preflight.note: off-camera: the page loads sidebar-collapsed then JS applies the profile persisted preference (~1-2s later). Settle before the railToggle normalization so the unless-check + press act on the settled state (a blind early click can over-toggle an already-expanded sidebar and fail its sidebarExpanded postcondition).

off-camera: stage the shared persisted sidebar-panel preference expanded so the MAINTENANCE group links read with labels. .wc-rail-panel-toggle is the same control both ways (probe-verified July 11: collapsed 'Show panel' at [25,936] -> click -> 'Hide panel', aside width 1 -> 220, five maint- links at x102 w203). Skipped when already expanded. Live default in the shared profile was collapsed.

ops.note: start page identity before capture (startUrl maint-issues.html — the WC-08-01 Issues list, reached in-app; its direct URL loads, unlike the four target pages)
pre-roll: preflight staged the persisted sidebar panel expanded; abort the take if collapsed
loaded: on Issues, KPI band present, all five MAINTENANCE links visible, no 404/error state
enter from the bottom edge and settle beside the MAINTENANCE rail as p01 says 'Start in Operations and look down the sidebar'
p01 tail / p02: rest beside the MAINTENANCE group; never on the nav links
p03-p04: hold at the top of the group as the three groups (Maintenance, Incident Reports, Scheduler) and the five entries are named
step 1 (p05): 'The first is Issues, the work-order list you already know' — beside the Issues entry (WC-08-01)
step 1 (p06): 'The four below it' — begin the slow top-to-bottom sweep of the four covered entries
step 1 (p07): 'the routines and gear and parts that sit behind every ticket'
p08 tail 'That set is today's tour' — ease off the rail
step 2 approach at the p09 cue 'Click Maintenance Schedule'
step 2: genuine in-app sidebar navigation (read-only, opens a list; the direct URL 404s, only the sidebar route loads it). Press inside the p09 trailing gap (cue ends 28.94, p10 starts 29.63)
load check: on Maintenance Schedule, 4 KPI tiles, Next Due column, rows, no error; abort otherwise
step 2 read-only hold: the Maintenance Schedule page never changes — no row opened, no task edited, nothing created
step 2 (p11): slow left-to-right sweep of the four KPI tiles (42 / 6 / 2 / 18)
step 2 (p12-p13): 'Each row is a job that repeats' — the Lubricate conveyor chain / Inspect chemical pumps rows
step 2 (p14): the NEXT DUE column header — the column a manager reads first; camera punches in
the Lubricate row's Next Due (2025-06-01)
step 2 guardrail (p15): rest BESIDE + New Record while the red do-not-click ring marks + New Record and Export; adding or editing a scheduled task mutates a live calendar, so they are never pressed
step 3 approach at the p16 cue 'Now Equipment Registry'
step 3: genuine in-app sidebar navigation (read-only; direct URL 404s). Press inside the p16 trailing gap (cue ends 54.02, p17 starts 54.40)
load check: on Equipment Registry, 4 tiles, Status column, rows, no error
step 3 read-only hold: the Equipment Registry page never changes — no asset row opened or edited
step 3 (p17-p18): land on the KPI band — four total assets, three operational, one in repair, zero retired
step 3 (p19): the four assets named across the NAME column (blower system, chemical pump array, main tunnel conveyor, POS terminal)
step 3 (p20): 'Three are operational and one sits in repair' — the STATUS column; camera punches in
the Chemical Pump Array 'in repair' status cell
step 3 (p21): 'When a caller says the air dryer is down' — hold on the flagged unit
step 3 (p22): rest on the in-repair row; the row itself is never clicked
step 3 guardrail: rest BESIDE + New Record while the red do-not-click ring marks + New Record and Export; creating or editing an asset is a mutation, never pressed
step 4 approach at the p23 cue 'Open Checklists'
step 4: genuine in-app sidebar navigation (read-only; direct URL 404s). Press inside the p23 trailing 1.4s break after 'Open Checklists' (block onset 76.59), well before the p25 detail line
load check: on Maintenance Checklists, 4 tiles, Completion % column, rows, no error
step 4 read-only hold: the Checklists page never changes — no checklist opened, run, or completed
step 4 (p24): land on the KPI band (24 / 8 / 94% / 1)
step 4 (p25): 'Daily opening and closing walks, a weekly safety inspection, a monthly deep clean' — sweep the rows
step 4 (p26): the COMPLETION % column; camera punches in
Daily Opening reads 100%
step 4 (p27): 'the average sits at ninety four percent' — the Avg Completion tile
step 4 guardrail (p28): rest BESIDE + New Record while the red do-not-click ring marks + New Record and Export; running or completing a checklist is a mutation, never pressed
step 4 (p29-p30): 'not the Lube Checklists page over in Hardware settings' — ease onto the list body while the on-screen caution note shows
step 5 approach at the p31 cue 'Last one. Parts and Supplies'
step 5: genuine in-app sidebar navigation (read-only; direct URL 404s). Press inside the p31 trailing gap (cue ends 105.07, p32 starts 105.75)
load check: on Parts & Supplies, 4 tiles, Qty on Hand column, rows, no error
step 5 read-only hold: the Parts & Supplies page never changes — no part opened, ordered, or edited
step 5 (p32): land on the KPI band
step 5 (p33): 'Drive belts, pump seal kits, dryer filters, a spare power supply' — sweep the PRT rows
step 5 (p34): '142 parts' — the Total Parts tile
'about eighteen thousand dollars' — the Total Value tile
step 5 (p35): 'Read quantity on hand against the reorder point' — the QTY ON HAND header; camera punches in
the REORDER PT header beside it
step 5 (p36): 'answer a do we have it call in seconds' — PRT-001 reads 4 on hand
reorder at 2
step 5 guardrail: rest BESIDE + New Record while the red do-not-click ring marks + New Record and Export; adding or ordering a part is a mutation, never pressed
step 5 (p37): 'keep it straight from Chemicals and Supplies over in Inventory' — ease onto the list while the separate-from-Inventory note shows
p38 tail 'this one is upkeep'
step 6 approach at the p39 cue 'So, the map' — return to Issues to show the whole MAINTENANCE group
step 6: genuine in-app sidebar navigation back to Issues (read-only). Press inside the p39 trailing gap (cue ends 133.29, p40 starts 134.02)
recap load check: back on Issues, KPI band and all five MAINTENANCE links present
recap needs the five MAINTENANCE links on screen
step 6 read-only hold: the recap stays on Issues; nothing was created or edited on any of the four toured pages
step 6 (p40): 'Four live pages under Maintenance, right beside Issues' — beside the Issues anchor
step 6 recap, spoken order (p41): 'A recurring job lands on the Schedule, and Next Due says when'
p42: 'A question about a machine goes to the Registry'
p43: 'The steps a tech follows are the Checklists'
p44: 'The parts a repair burns through are Parts and Supplies'
p45 'One habit before you go' — ease down the rail
step 6 (p46): 'Reach these pages from the sidebar inside Operations' — gesture up the whole group
p47: 'typing an address straight into the browser still fails' — the sidebar is the front door, not the URL bar
p48 'The sidebar is the front door' — final park, clear of content and captions as the close lands
end state: still on Issues, unchanged; nothing was created or edited on any of the four maintenance pages
